div.head: Memorandum of Telephone Conversation, by the Chief of the Division of Near Eastern Affairs (Alling)
div.p:
Mr. Saleh telephoned from New York to say that he had received a telegram from the Minister of Finance reading as follows:
“Please insist that wheat be shipped to Iran with the greatest rapidity.”

I told Mr. Saleh that we were going into the whole question of supplies for Iran, and we hoped that a satisfactory solution could be found in the very near future. He volunteered the idea that it would be well for our Legation at Tehran to concert with the Iranian Government
authorities and with the British authorities in arranging a supply program. I told him I was very glad to have this suggestion and that we would look into it.
